message:
fix(fnx-algorithms): convert dijkstra debug scratchpad to real test

br-r37-c1-btmc5: crates/fnx-algorithms/src/test_dijkstra.rs was
checked in as a debug-print scratchpad with the canonical anti-
pattern `assert!(false); // to see the output`. That pattern fails
the test on every run — exactly what cargo test --release-mode
quality gates are supposed to catch — but it had been silently
masked by the file's name (test_dijkstra is a module name, not a
test fn name) and by the test landing under #[test] only after
the assert!(false).

Convert to a real regression test:

- Rename test_directed_multi → multi_source_dijkstra_directed_
  returns_zero_for_source_and_one_for_successor (descriptive
  enough that the test name documents what it pins).
- Drop the println! debug calls.
- Drop the assert!(false).
- Add real assertions on the returned distances:
  - distance to "b" (the source) is 0.0
  - distance to "c" (the immediate successor) is 1.0
  - "a" does not appear in distances (unreachable from "b" in
    the directed chain a→b→c — proves the algorithm respects
    edge direction)
- Use a HashMap<&str, f64> view of res.distances so the assertions
  are robust to insertion order in the result vector.

Now the test passes on green, fails loudly if multi_source_dijkstra_
directed silently includes unreachable nodes or computes the wrong
distance, and the file is no longer a quality-gate liability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
